fault_hypothesis: Lines 74-75 incorrectly pop the last element when both left and right end with the same value, but this is wrong — all elements from both inputs should be preserved. The condition and pop on line 75 should not exist.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition on line 74 should never be true because no element should be removed from the merged result; when it is true on failing tests, the subsequent pop corrupts the output.
Right after initialization merged must be empty.
Execution should never enter the then-branch of line 74 because no element should be popped from merged; this branch is always wrong.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as the sum of both input lengths.
